metric_value: Average cost to manage a single networked PC is 2x to 3x its original purchase price
notes: Key TCO finding from IS executive interviews

metric_value: HP expects to reduce desktop management costs by at least 20-25% (approximately $3,000 per PC) in the near term
notes: HP's own cost reduction claim

metric_value: Aberdeen projects HP DMI-based management combined with best practices could reduce total cost of PC ownership by 50% or more
notes: Aberdeen's higher-than-HP estimate; conditional on best practice adoption

metric_value: HP added approximately 300 DMI manageable attributes plus 65 unique HP DMI attribute extensions to Vectra PC line
notes: DMI implementation breadth on Vectra Corporate PCs

metric_value: HP Vectra Corporate PC features more than 350 total DMI attributes
notes: 350 total DMI attributes claimed by HP for Vectra

metric_value: HP was the first major supplier to introduce DMI-compliant PCs starting in 1992
notes: HP's first-mover advantage in DMI compliance

metric_value: DMTF has more than 122 PC hardware and software supplier members beyond steering committee
notes: Breadth of DMI standard adoption

metric_value: DMI certification will become a de facto industry standard for desktop management; Aberdeen expects DMI to be a major competitive differentiator in 1997
notes: Core Aberdeen prediction on DMI standardization

metric_value: DMI reached end-of-life on March 31 2005; superseded by CIM and Windows Management Instrumentation (WMI); DMTF ended active development December 31 2003
notes: DMI was a de facto standard briefly but WMI/CIM dominated long term; DMTF source: https://www.dmtf.org/content/dmtf-announces-end-life-dmi

metric_value: HP will position its DMI-based desktop management environment as a major differentiator to surpass Compaq in enterprise PC sales
notes: Core competitive prediction

metric_value: HP merged with Compaq in 2002 for $25 billion; Vectra line discontinued; replaced by HP Evo (based on Compaq platform); DMI competition moot
notes: HP did not overtake Compaq through DMI — they acquired Compaq; Wikipedia: HP Vectra discontinued 2002

metric_value: Compaq Intelligent Manageability is described as a proprietary solution requiring Compaq hardware and an invitation program for third-party ISVs
notes: Aberdeen's characterization of Compaq's approach as proprietary barrier

metric_value: HP Vectra BIOS flash capability allows IS managers to extend PC life by 2 years beyond typical 3-year major technology cycles
notes: Claim about Vectra's unique remote multi-PC BIOS upgrade capability

metric_value: NightDIRECTOR uses Magic Packet technology (co-developed with AMD) to remotely power on PCs for maintenance without disturbing end users
notes: NightDIRECTOR capability description

metric_value: Magic Packet technology evolved into Wake-on-LAN (WOL) standard; still widely supported in enterprise PCs and servers as of 2024
notes: Wake-on-LAN became the de facto standard; this prediction effectively came true

metric_value: HP OpenView Professional Suite manages small-to-medium companies with 10-2,000 nodes; OpenView Network Node Manager supports LANs and WANs with more than 250 nodes
notes: OpenView product tier descriptions

metric_value: IS managers require three things: better data integration between management environments; better automation of manual tasks; better PC manageability especially remote configuration
notes: Three requirements framework from IS executive interviews

metric_value: DMTF sponsors annual DMI compliance bake-off to certify supplier compliance; certification is a competitive differentiator
notes: DMTF compliance testing process description

metric_value: HP TopTOOLS is the only product capable of upgrading BIOS of multiple PCs simultaneously from a central console
notes: Claimed unique HP capability for remote multi-BIOS flash upgrade

metric_value: TopTOOLS includes AssetTOOLS (inventory), ConfigTOOLS (configuration/troubleshoot), FlashTOOL (remote BIOS upgrade), SafeTOOLS (health monitoring), ProtectTOOLS (security)
notes: TopTOOLS product suite composition

metric_value: IS buyers have made DMI adherence a checklist item for PC purchases; failure to implement means no sale
notes: Aberdeen's characterization of DMI as purchase requirement

metric_value: Vectra PC platform prices declined significantly in 1996 due to CPU and memory price reductions
notes: Hardware cost reduction context

metric_value: IS managers have PCs on 3-5 year tax depreciation schedules; need to extend life by 2 years beyond 3-year major technology cycles
notes: Business context for remote management and BIOS upgrade value proposition

metric_value: HP OpenView rebranded as HP Software Division products in 2007 after acquisitions of Mercury Interactive and Peregrine Systems; eventually sold to MicroFocus; now part of OpenText
notes: HP OpenView Wikipedia: rebranded 2007; HPE Software sold to MicroFocus

metric_value: Microsoft shipped Windows Management Instrumentation (WMI) with Windows 2000; became the dominant desktop management API; effectively superseding DMI in enterprise environments
notes: WMI built on CIM; Microsoft's implementation became the standard; not DMI itself
